diagrams.title: Relational Model Structure - Chapters Table
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a relational table with key components labeled
elements: Column Headers (Attributes): ChapterID, ChapterTitle, Pages, BookID
Foreign Key Column: BookID (references Books table)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the basic structure of a relation with tuples, attributes, and key identification

diagrams.title: Keys Hierarchy in Relational Model
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between different types of keys from most general to most specific
elements: Outer Circle Label: SUPER KEY (any combination that identifies uniquely)
Middle Circle Inside: CANDIDATE KEY (minimal super key, no subset is key)
Inner Circle: PRIMARY KEY (chosen candidate key)
Arrows showing: Super Key → contains → Candidate Key → contains → Primary Key
Examples shown at each level: {ChapterID, Title} → {ChapterID} → ChapterID
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the containment relationship between super key, candidate key, and primary key

diagrams.title: Referential Integrity - Foreign Key Relationship
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how foreign key establishes relationship between two tables
elements: Row shows: ChapterID, ChapterTitle, BookID column with value 101
Arrow from Chapters.BookID pointing to Books.BookID
Label on arrow: 'Foreign Key references Primary Key'
Note: 'Every BookID in Chapters must exist in Books OR be NULL'
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how referential integrity is maintained through foreign key constraints
